codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all key requirements. The POST /api/discovery/analyze endpoint correctly validates input with Zod, sanitizes for XSS, routes to mock/real AI, handles timeout and errors with friendly messages, and returns structured talent analysis. The OpenAI client is well-designed with mock toggle, detailed system prompt emphasizing deep analysis, AbortController-based timeout, and Zod output validation. UI components (AnalysisResults, AnalysisLoading, AnalysisError) are clean, accessible (ARIA roles/labels), bilingual, and child-friendly. Test coverage is comprehensive: 10 API tests, 5 client tests, 16 component tests covering happy paths, error states, XSS, timeouts, and accessibility. Two minor non-blocking gaps noted: (1) the feature description mentions analysisId in the Zod response schema, but the analysis endpoint returns only { talents } — the ID is generated downstream by the save endpoint, which is a reasonable architectural choice but deviates from the literal spec; (2) artifactUrl uses z.string().min(1) without z.url() validation, relying on XSS pattern detection instead. Neither is blocking.

issues.description: Feature description specifies 'Zod validation on response (talents array, analysisId)' but AnalysisOutputSchema only contains { talents }. No analysisId field exists in the response. The ID is generated later by the /api/discovery/save endpoint, so functionally this works, but the schema deviates from the literal feature spec.

artifactUrl is validated as z.string().min(1) — any non-empty string passes Zod validation, not just URLs. The XSS pattern check in the route handler provides a defense layer, but z.string().url() would be a stronger first-line validation. This is mitigated by the containsSuspiciousPatterns check.

Image mock response selection uses Date.now() % 2 for rotation, which means tests are non-deterministic for the image path — some test runs get engineering, others get artistic. This hasn't caused test failures because the tests import mockVariants directly rather than calling getMockMultimodalAnalysis for image assertions, but it could cause confusion if a future test relies on the image mock path returning a specific variant.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description mentions 'talent category' as part of analysis results, but the TalentSchema only has name/confidence/reasoning — no explicit category field. The talent name effectively serves as the category. This implicit convention (name-as-category) is not documented in AGENTS.md or architecture.md, which could lead to confusion when a downstream feature (e.g., GalleryEntry.talentCategory from the data model) needs to map analysis results to categories.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/ai/schemas.ts TalentSchema has { name, confidence, reasoning }. architecture.md shows GalleryEntry has a talentCategory field. Feature description says 'talent category' should be in results.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The analyze route uses console.error for error logging, consistent with all other API routes in the project (auth/login, auth/codes, upload/complete, discovery/save, etc.). This is a de facto convention but not documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/discovery/analyze/route.ts:64, same pattern in discovery/save/route.ts:84, auth/login/route.ts:106, upload/presigned-url/route.ts:71, etc.

summary: Feature passes review. The implementation is comprehensive and well-structured: POST /api/discovery/analyze with auth, Zod validation, XSS prevention, GPT-4o mock layer with 3 realistic variants showing deep talent detection (engineering/artistic/narrative), timeout handling with AbortController, friendly error messages, and accessible bilingual UI components. 31 tests cover API, client, and component layers thoroughly. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) analysisId mentioned in feature spec is absent from the response schema (generated downstream by save endpoint instead), (2) artifactUrl lacks z.url() validation (mitigated by XSS pattern check). The worker followed the ai-feature-worker skill procedure correctly.
